title: Development Guide
text: Developer conventions implementation notes evfuse package.

title: Development Guide
text: Parameter-major ordering throughout (parameter index varies fastest): index (j-1)*L + param j site Log-rho parameterization optimization (ensures rho > 0) Lambda = 300 km Wendland C4 (order-2) taper bootstrap W B = 500 bootstrap replicates 20 multi-start random restarts log-uniform[50, 5000] km Prediction grid: filtered coastal points 15 km spacing, lon -98 -66, lat 24 46 Seeds: bootstrap (42), retry loop (101-105), multi-start (2026), return level simulation (123) source_params abstraction: named list mapping source labels parameter indices (e.g., list(NOAA = 1:3, ADCIRC = 4:6)); core functions accept via dat$source_params Unicode labels figures (μ₀, ξ, σ) require ragg::agg_png device ggsave

title: Development Guide
text: extRemes::fevd returns observed information matrix directly (positive definite), Hessian negative log-likelihood. Use solve(fit$results$hessian) directly vcov. Delta method log_sigma transformation: J = diag(1, 1/sigma, 1) Rho optimized log space: pack_params stores log(rho), unpack_params exponentiates. Gradient uses chain rule. Analytic gradient contracts Q = V_inv - alpha*alpha^T Omega_i pxp H matrices. See Section 3.3 Supporting Information Section S1 (Eqs S1-S4). Sigma_obs built directly observed space (never form full Lp x Lp). nll/nll_grad share cache keyed parameter vector avoid redundant Cholesky. core functions generalized via source_params; defaults match NOAA/ADCIRC case. wendland2() implements Wendland C4 (psi_{2,2} Furrer et al. 2006), C2.

title: Development Guide
text: Two nonstationary Stage 1 variants available: Linear time trend (fit_gev_detrended): fits μ(t) = μ₀ + μ₁(t − t_ref) NOAA sites, discards μ₁ passes (μ₀, log σ, ξ) Stage 2. Used primary manuscript analysis. Covariate sensitivity (fit_gev_ns): fits μ(t) = μ₀ + μ₁·covariate(t), retains μ₁ θ̂ (4 columns). Supports asymmetric source_params (e.g., list(NOAA = 1:4, ADCIRC = 5:7) p=7 model 42 optimization parameters). Used forecasting extension. Key implementation details covariate pipeline: - bootstrap_W_ns: Gamma uses union ordering (4 params/site); ADCIRC μ₁ positions NA → cov() produces NA - embed_W: detects unobserved params via .na() W_tap diagonal, maps observed entries - taper_W must use p = 4 (default 3) nonstationary bootstrap - compute_return_levels_ns: 4-dim delta method gradient via chain rule

title: Development Guide
text: Source: data(coast_data) data-raw/combined_max.csv 129 sites: 29 NOAA, 100 ADCIRC 34-43 years annual maxima per site

title: Development Guide
text: Iterate devtools::load_all() devtools::test() Run tests every change (102 tests tests/testthat/test-core.R) R CMD check pass 0 errors, 0 notes (2 warnings vignette inst/doc pandoc unavailable expected)

title: Development Guide
code: R/                       Core package source (13 files) scripts/                 Analysis and reproduction scripts   run_nonstationary.R    Master script: reproduces all paper results   simulation_study.R     Parameter recovery under non-co-location (Table 8)   rmse_decomposition.R   RMSE decomposition by parameter and region (Table 3)   baseline_comparisons.R Nearest-ADCIRC bias correction baselines (§5.1)   run_trends.R           Trend diagnostics (trend map)   plot_study_area.R      Study area map (Figure 1)   gradient_benchmark.R   Analytic vs numerical gradient timing comparison   supplementary/         Stationary pipeline, sensitivity, and exploratory analyses data/                    Bundled package data (coast_data.rda) data-raw/                Source data, data preparation, computed artifacts tables/                  Summary tables (regenerated by run_nonstationary.R) figures/                 Output figures (19 PNGs) tests/testthat/          Unit tests (102 tests) vignettes/               Tutorial vignette

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: evfuse package implements two-stage frequentist framework fusing sparse observations dense simulations spatial extreme value analysis (White et al., preparation). developed U.S. coastal sea levels (NOAA tidal gauges + ADCIRC simulations), framework applies setting annual maxima multiple spatial data sources. Stage 1: Fit GEV distributions independently site. Stage 2: Fit joint multivariate Gaussian process models cross-source dependence via coregionalization (LMC) structure. Kriging: Predict new locations, drawing information sources even source observes subset parameters. Return levels: Estimate r-year return levels uncertainty via Monte Carlo simulation /delta method.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: package includes coast_data, dataset annual maximum sea levels 129 sites along U.S. Gulf Atlantic coasts: 29 NOAA tidal gauge stations 100 ADCIRC numerical simulation points.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: Fit GEV(mu, sigma, xi) site via maximum likelihood. Parameters stored (mu, log sigma, xi) unconstrained optimization.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: Estimate covariance Stage 1 MLEs via nonparametric block bootstrap (resampling years preserve spatial dependence within data source). Apply Wendland tapering enforce sparsity.
code: D <- compute_distances(coast_data$sites) bs <- bootstrap_W(coast_data, B = 500, seed = 42) W_tap <- taper_W(bs$W_bs, D, lambda = 300)

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: joint model p=6 dimensions (3 per source) parameters: beta (6x1): Mean vector (mu_NOAA, log_sigma_NOAA, xi_NOAA, mu_ADCIRC, log_sigma_ADCIRC, xi_ADCIRC). (6x6 lower triangular): Cross-covariance factor inter-parameter covariance distance 0 ^T. rho (6x1): Range parameters 6 latent exponential correlation functions. observed parameter vector theta_hat ~ N(beta x 1_L, Sigma + W_tap), site contributes observed dimensions (NOAA: 1-3, ADCIRC: 4-6).
code: model <- fit_spatial_model(stage1, coast_data, W_tap, D,                            control = list(maxit = 2000, trace = 0)) model$optim_result$convergence # [1] 0

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: Predict new locations. kriging predictor fuses information NOAA ADCIRC observations produce NOAA-scale GEV parameters reduced uncertainty.
code: # Predict at a few example coastal sites new_sites <- data.frame(lon = c(-90.0, -81.5, -75.5), lat = c(30.0, 31.5, 36.8)) preds <- predict_krig(model, new_sites) rl <- compute_return_levels(preds, r = 100)  # 100-year return levels at year-2000 reference conditions rl$return_level

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: closed-form block LOO-CV (Rasmussen & Williams, 2006, eq. 5.12) evaluates predictive performance NOAA site without refitting.
code: loo <- loo_cv(model) sum_loo <- loo_summary(loo, r = 100)  # Total log predictive density sum_loo$total_lpd  # 100-year return level RMSE sum_loo$rl_rmse

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels
text: source_params argument generalizes framework beyond NOAA/ADCIRC. entry maps data source name parameter indices joint model:
code: # Example: 3 sources, each observing 3 GEV parameters dat <- load_data(df, source_params = list(   gauge    = 1:3,   satellite = 4:6,   reanalysis = 7:9 ))  # Fit a 9-dimensional joint model model <- fit_spatial_model(stage1, dat, W_tap, D)

what: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: Fusing sparse observations dense simulations spatial extreme value analysis. Implements two-stage frequentist framework : White, B. N., Blanton, B., Luettich, R., & Smith, R. L. Fusing Sparse Observations Dense Simulations Spatial Extreme Value Analysis: Application U.S. Coastal Sea Levels. preparation. package developed fusing NOAA tidal gauge observations ADCIRC hydrodynamic simulations coastal sea levels, framework general: application annual maxima multiple spatial data sources (observed, modeled, satellite, reanalysis, etc.) can use evfuse specifying source--parameter mapping via source_params.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: Applied 129 sites along U.S. Gulf Atlantic coasts (29 NOAA tide gauges + 100 ADCIRC simulation points): estimated cross-source correlations high: 0.995 location (μ₀), 0.443 log-scale (log σ), 0.837 shape (ξ). correlations quantify agreement NOAA observations ADCIRC simulations mechanism data fusion improves estimation. Mean standard errors drop 10%, largest gains isolated stations far gauges.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: Requires R >= 3.5. Dependencies (extRemes, Matrix) installed automatically.

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: detailed walkthrough including model diagnostics, custom data sources, source_params abstraction, see tutorial vignette. can also view R installing vignettes:
code: library(evfuse)  # Bundled dataset: 29 NOAA + 100 ADCIRC sites along U.S. Gulf/Atlantic coasts data(coast_data) D <- compute_distances(coast_data$sites)  # Stage 1: Fit GEV at each site stage1 <- fit_gev_all(coast_data)  # Bootstrap measurement uncertainty bs <- bootstrap_W(coast_data, B = 500, seed = 42) W_tap <- taper_W(bs$W_bs, D, lambda = 300)  # Stage 2: Fit 6-dim joint GP model model <- fit_spatial_model(stage1, coast_data, W_tap, D)  # Predict at new locations (data frame with lon, lat columns) new_sites <- data.frame(lon = c(-90.0, -81.5), lat = c(30.0, 31.5)) preds <- predict_krig(model, new_sites) rl <- compute_return_levels(preds, r = 100)  # 100-year return levels at year-2000 reference conditions rl$return_level rl$se_sim     # Monte Carlo simulation SEs (recommended) rl$se_delta   # delta method SEs (closed-form check)  # Optional: compare against a single-source baseline via LOO-CV # (closed-form, no refitting required) loo <- loo_cv(model) sum_loo <- loo_summary(loo, r = 100)  # r = return period in years sum_loo$total_lpd    # log predictive density (higher = better) sum_loo$rl_rmse      # 100-year return level RMSE devtools::install_github("BrianNathanWhite/evfuse", build_vignettes = TRUE) vignette("evfuse-tutorial")

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: Stage 1: Fit GEV(μ, log σ, ξ) independently site via maximum likelihood (fit_gev_all()). Bootstrap: Estimate measurement error covariance via nonparametric block bootstrap (resampling years within source), apply Wendland C4 tapering regularize (bootstrap_W(), taper_W()). Stage 2: Estimate p-dimensional linear model coregionalization (mean vector β, cross-covariance factor , range parameters ρ) via optim analytic gradients (fit_spatial_model()). selection matrix accommodates partial observations, site provides estimates one source. Kriging: Predict unobserved locations, exploiting information data sources even though site observes subset p parameters (predict_krig()). Return levels: Confidence intervals r-year return levels via Monte Carlo simulation delta method (compute_return_levels()).

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: input data frame needs columns: lon, lat, location, year, max_sea_level, data_source: row one annual maximum one site. Sites must belong exactly one data source. two networks overlap location, include site twice distinct location names (e.g. "site_A_gauge" "site_A_model") coordinates; zero-distance covariance correctly captures cross-source dependence. source_params argument maps source labels parameter indices joint model: source contributes 3 GEV parameters (μ, log σ, ξ) per site. framework jointly models sources, learning cross-source correlations allow dense potentially biased simulations improve estimation sparse observation locations.
code: # df is your data frame in the format shown above # 2 sources (default): 6-dim model dat <- load_data(df, source_params = list(gauge = 1:3, model = 4:6))  # 3 sources: 9-dim model dat <- load_data(df, source_params = list(   gauge      = 1:3,   satellite  = 4:6,   reanalysis = 7:9 ))  # Then run the full pipeline D <- compute_distances(dat$sites) stage1 <- fit_gev_all(dat) bs <- bootstrap_W(dat, B = 500, seed = 42) W_tap <- taper_W(bs$W_bs, D, lambda = 300) model <- fit_spatial_model(stage1, dat, W_tap, D)  # Predict at new sites (kriging returns parameters from the first source by default) preds <- predict_krig(model, new_sites) rl <- compute_return_levels(preds, r = 100)

title: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis
text: figures, tables, numerical results paper generated single master script. Clone repository run: runs full nonstationary pipeline end--end: Stage 1 GEV fitting (linear μ trend NOAA sites), bootstrap, Stage 2 coregionalization, kriging, return level maps, LOO-CV, block CV, PIT calibration, taper sensitivity, manuscript figures. Expected runtime approximately 15 minutes modern desktop. bundled coast_data dataset contains 129 sites x 43 years annual maxima needed reproduce analysis. Output goes figures/ (PNGs), tables/ (summary text), data-raw/ (fitted model objects .rds). Additional scripts produce specific figures analyses. Scripts marked * can run independently; rest require run_nonstationary.R first: Seeds stochastic steps documented scripts fixed reproducibility (bootstrap: 42, multi-start optimization: 2026).
code: git clone https://github.com/BrianNathanWhite/evfuse.git cd evfuse Rscript scripts/run_nonstationary.R Rscript scripts/run_trends.R           # Trend diagnostics (trend map) * Rscript scripts/plot_study_area.R      # Study area map (Figure 1) * Rscript scripts/simulation_study.R     # Parameter recovery simulation study (§4.6.4) Rscript scripts/rmse_decomposition.R   # RL improvement by parameter and region (Table 3) Rscript scripts/baseline_comparisons.R # Nearest-ADCIRC bias correction baselines (§5.1) Rscript scripts/gradient_benchmark.R   # Analytic vs numerical gradient timing

what: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
title: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
text: Implements bootstrap procedure Section 2.4 Russell et al. (2020). Resamples years (preserving spatial dependence) re-fits stationary GEV site, estimates covariance stage-1 estimators. nonstationary alternative accounts NOAA sea level trends, see bootstrap_W_detrended.

title: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
code: bootstrap_W(dat, B = 500, log_scale = TRUE, seed = NULL)

title: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
text: dat evfuse_data object. B Number bootstrap replications (default 500). log_scale Logical. TRUE, work log(sigma) (default TRUE). seed Random seed reproducibility.

title: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
text: list components: W_bs raw bootstrap covariance matrix (Lp x Lp). Gamma bootstrap matrix MLEs (B x Lp). n_failures Number (site, bootstrap) combinations failed.

title: Estimate W via nonparametric block bootstrap (stationary) — bootstrap_W
text: small fraction bootstrap resamples may produce degenerate data causes warnings convergence failures underlying GEV fitting routine (extRemes::fevd). expected behavior: failed fits recorded n_failures handled via pairwise-complete covariance estimation. Typical failure rates well 1 percent.

what: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
title: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
text: Like bootstrap_W NOAA sites fitted nonstationary GEV (linear mu trend), extracting (mu0, log_sigma, xi) per replicate. ADCIRC sites use standard stationary GEV. primary bootstrap approach used manuscript.

title: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
code: bootstrap_W_detrended(dat, df, B = 500, ref_year = 2000, seed = NULL)

title: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
text: dat evfuse_data object. df raw data frame year column. B Number bootstrap replications. ref_year Reference year centering. seed Random seed.

title: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
text: list W_bs, Gamma, n_failures.

title: Bootstrap W with NOAA detrending — bootstrap_W_detrended
text: small fraction bootstrap resamples may produce degenerate data causes warnings convergence failures underlying GEV fitting routine (extRemes::fevd). expected behavior: failed fits recorded n_failures handled via pairwise-complete covariance estimation. Typical failure rates well 1 percent.

what: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
text: Like bootstrap_W_detrended uses arbitrary covariate (e.g., SST) instead linear time trend, retains covariate sensitivity mu1 bootstrap parameter vector. NOAA sites fitted nonstationary GEV; ADCIRC sites use stationary GEV.

title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
code: bootstrap_W_ns(dat, df, covariate, B = 500, ref_value = NULL, seed = NULL)

title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
text: dat evfuse_data object. df raw data frame covariate column. covariate String column name df. B Number bootstrap replications. ref_value Centering value (default: mean across NOAA site-years). seed Random seed.

title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
text: list W_bs (4L x 4L), Gamma (B x 4L), n_failures.

title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
text: bootstrap Gamma matrix L * 4 columns parameter-major ordering: (mu0, mu1, log_sigma, xi) sites. ADCIRC mu1 entries NA; resulting W_bs NA positions. design: embed_W skips unobserved entries.

title: Bootstrap W with nonstationary covariate — bootstrap_W_ns
text: small fraction bootstrap resamples may produce degenerate data causes warnings convergence failures underlying GEV fitting routine (extRemes::fevd). expected behavior: failed fits recorded n_failures handled via pairwise-complete covariance estimation. Typical failure rates well 1 percent.

what: Compute return levels with confidence intervals — compute_return_levels

text: Takes kriging predictions (NOAA GEV parameters covariance) computes r-year return levels 95% confidence intervals via delta method /simulation predictive distribution.

title: Compute return levels with confidence intervals — compute_return_levels
text: data frame columns: lon, lat Coordinates prediction sites. return_level Point estimate r-year return level. se_delta Standard error delta method (computed). ci_lower_delta, ci_upper_delta Delta method CI bounds. se_sim Standard error simulation (computed). ci_lower_sim, ci_upper_sim Simulation-based CI bounds.

title: Embed W_tap into full parameter space — embed_W
text: sources observe different numbers parameters (e.g., NOAA 4 ADCIRC 3 nonstationary case), bootstrap uses union parameter ordering NA entries unobserved positions. function detects unobserved positions NA diagonal W_tap maps observed entries.

what: evfuse: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis — evfuse-package
title: evfuse: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis — evfuse-package
text: Two-stage frequentist framework fusing sparse observations dense simulations spatial extreme value analysis. Fits generalized extreme value (GEV) distributions independently site, models site-level parameter estimates jointly via linear model coregionalization selection matrix accommodates partial observations. Developed U.S. coastal sea levels (White et al.) applicable setting annual maxima multiple spatial data sources can fused.

title: evfuse: Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Value Analysis — evfuse-package
text: Maintainer: Brian N. White bnw@unc.edu Authors: Brian Blanton Rick Luettich Richard L. Smith

title: Exponential covariance matrix — exp_cov_matrix
text: Computes correlation matrix Omega_i Omega_i(j,k) = exp(-D(j,k) / rho) range parameter rho > 0.

title: Exponential covariance matrix — exp_cov_matrix
Exponential covariance matrix — exp_cov_matrix
text: D Distance matrix. rho Range parameter (positive).

title: Exponential covariance matrix — exp_cov_matrix
text: Correlation matrix dimension D.

what: Fit GEV at all sites (Stage 1, stationary) — fit_gev_all
title: Fit GEV at all sites (Stage 1, stationary) — fit_gev_all
text: Fits stationary GEV distribution annual block maxima site using extRemes::fevd. Returns pointwise MLEs asymptotic covariance matrices. nonstationary alternative accounts sea level trends NOAA sites, see fit_gev_detrended.

title: Fit GEV at all sites (Stage 1, stationary) — fit_gev_all
Fit GEV at all sites (Stage 1, stationary) — fit_gev_all
text: dat evfuse_data object load_data. log_scale Logical. TRUE (default), return log(sigma) instead sigma. standard stage 2 model need unconstrained parameters.

title: Fit GEV at all sites (Stage 1, stationary) — fit_gev_all
text: list components: theta_hat Matrix dimension (n_sites x 3). Columns mu, log_sigma (sigma), xi. Rows ordered match dat$sites. vcov_list List n_sites 3x3 asymptotic covariance matrices (mu, log_sigma, xi) site. fits List raw fevd fit objects diagnostics. converged Logical vector indicating convergence site.

what: Fit GEV at all sites with NOAA detrending (Stage 1, nonstationary) — fit_gev_detrended
title: Fit GEV at all sites with NOAA detrending (Stage 1, nonstationary) — fit_gev_detrended
text: NOAA sites get nonstationary GEV: mu(t) = mu0 + mu1*(t - ref_year), sigma xi stationary. detrended triplet (mu0, log_sigma, xi) returned. ADCIRC sites get usual stationary fit. primary Stage 1 approach used manuscript.

title: Fit GEV at all sites with NOAA detrending (Stage 1, nonstationary) — fit_gev_detrended
Fit GEV at all sites with NOAA detrending (Stage 1, nonstationary) — fit_gev_detrended
text: dat evfuse_data object. df raw data frame year column. ref_year Reference year centering (default 2000).

title: Fit GEV at all sites with NOAA detrending (Stage 1, nonstationary) — fit_gev_detrended
text: evfuse_stage1 object detrended NOAA parameters.

what: Fit nonstationary GEV with covariate (Stage 1) — fit_gev_ns
title: Fit nonstationary GEV with covariate (Stage 1) — fit_gev_ns
text: NOAA sites get nonstationary GEV: mu(t) = mu0 + mu1 * (cov(t) - ref_value), sigma xi stationary. four parameters (mu0, mu1, log_sigma, xi) returned spatial modeling. ADCIRC sites get usual stationary fit three parameters (mu0, log_sigma, xi).

title: Fit nonstationary GEV with covariate (Stage 1) — fit_gev_ns
Fit nonstationary GEV with covariate (Stage 1) — fit_gev_ns
text: dat evfuse_data object. df raw data frame containing covariate column. covariate String column name df (e.g., "sst_regional_warm"). ref_value Centering value covariate. Default: mean across NOAA site-years. Centering improves numerical stability makes mu0 interpretable location reference covariate value.

title: Fit nonstationary GEV with covariate (Stage 1) — fit_gev_ns
text: evfuse_stage1 object 4-column theta_hat. NOAA rows contain (mu0, mu1, log_sigma, xi); ADCIRC rows contain (mu0, log_sigma, xi, NA). vcov_list entries 4x4 NOAA 3x3 ADCIRC.

what: Fit a naive (single-source) 3-dim spatial model — fit_naive_model
title: Fit a naive (single-source) 3-dim spatial model — fit_naive_model
text: Fits 3-dimensional GP coregionalization data single source (NOAA ADCIRC ). sites observe 3 parameters, eliminating partial-observation complexity joint model.

title: Fit a naive (single-source) 3-dim spatial model — fit_naive_model
code: fit_naive_model(   stage1,   dat,   W_bs,   D,   source = c("NOAA", "ADCIRC"),   lambda = 300,   n_starts = 20,   control = list(maxit = 2000, trace = 1) )

title: Fit a naive (single-source) 3-dim spatial model — fit_naive_model
text: stage1 evfuse_stage1 object fit_gev_all fit_gev_detrended. dat evfuse_data object. W_bs Raw bootstrap covariance matrix (L3 x L3). D Distance matrix (L x L). source Character: "NOAA" "ADCIRC". lambda Wendland taper range km (default 300). n_starts Number multi-start attempts (default 20). control Control list passed optim.

title: Fit a naive (single-source) 3-dim spatial model — fit_naive_model
text: evfuse_naive_model object.

what: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
title: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
text: Estimates beta (6x1), (6x6 lower triangular), rho (6x1) maximizing Gaussian likelihood Eq. (7) Russell et al. (2020), extended handle partial observations. NOAA sites observe components 1-3 ADCIRC sites observe components 4-6 6-dimensional parameter vector.

title: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
code: fit_spatial_model(   stage1,   dat,   W_tap,   D,   start = NULL,   method = "L-BFGS-B",   control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 1),   check_gradient = FALSE )

title: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
text: stage1 evfuse_stage1 object fit_gev_all fit_gev_detrended. dat evfuse_data object. W_tap Tapered covariance matrix taper_W. D Distance matrix compute_distances. start Optional named list starting values beta, , rho. method Optimization method optim (default "L-BFGS-B"). control Control list passed optim. check_gradient TRUE, verify analytic gradient numerical finite differences starting values optimizing.

title: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
text: list components: beta Estimated mean vector (6x1). Estimated lower triangular matrix (6x6). rho Estimated range parameters (6x1). Sigma Estimated covariance matrix observed sites. optim_result Raw output optim. grad_check check_gradient = TRUE, output verify_gradient.

title: Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE — fit_spatial_model
text: Uses analytic gradient derived coregionalization structure (see Sections 2, 5, 6 gradient notes). key identity dSigma/dA_ab dSigma/drho_k rank-1 Kronecker products, enabling O(n_obs^2) gradient computation per parameter instead O(n_obs^3).

title: GEV exceedance probability — gev_exceedance_prob
text: Computes P(Z > z0) Z ~ GEV(mu, sigma, xi).

title: GEV exceedance probability — gev_exceedance_prob
GEV exceedance probability — gev_exceedance_prob
text: z0 Threshold value. mu Location parameter. sigma Scale parameter (log scale). xi Shape parameter.

title: GEV exceedance probability — gev_exceedance_prob
text: Exceedance probability.

title: GEV quantile function (return level) — gev_return_level
text: Computes r-year return level GEV parameters.

title: GEV quantile function (return level) — gev_return_level
GEV quantile function (return level) — gev_return_level
text: mu Location parameter. sigma Scale parameter (log scale). xi Shape parameter. r Return period years.

title: GEV quantile function (return level) — gev_return_level
text: r-year return level.

what: GEV trend test via likelihood ratio — gev_trend_test
title: GEV trend test via likelihood ratio — gev_trend_test
text: Fits stationary GEV(mu, sigma, xi) nonstationary GEV(mu0 + mu1*year, sigma, xi) via extRemes::fevd, compares likelihood ratio test (chi-squared, df=1).

title: GEV trend test via likelihood ratio — gev_trend_test
GEV trend test via likelihood ratio — gev_trend_test
text: annual_maxima Numeric vector annual maxima. years Numeric vector corresponding years (length).

title: GEV trend test via likelihood ratio — gev_trend_test
text: list components: mu0 Intercept nonstationary location. mu1 Slope nonstationary location (units per year). lrt_stat Likelihood ratio test statistic. lrt_pvalue P-value chi-squared(df=1). aic_stat AIC stationary model. aic_nonstat AIC nonstationary model. nllh_stat Negative log-likelihood stationary model. nllh_nonstat Negative log-likelihood nonstationary model.

what: Load and validate sea level data — load_data
title: Load and validate sea level data — load_data
text: Reads data frame columns: lon, lat, location, year, max_sea_level, data_source. Validates required columns types, returns structured list ready stage-1 GEV fitting.

title: Load and validate sea level data — load_data
Load and validate sea level data — load_data
text: df data frame required columns. source_params Named list mapping data source labels parameter indices joint model. Default: list(NOAA = 1:3, ADCIRC = 4:6).

title: Load and validate sea level data — load_data
text: evfuse_data list components: sites Data frame unique sites lon, lat, location, data_source. maxima Named list numeric vectors annual maxima, keyed location. n_noaa Number NOAA sites. n_adcirc Number ADCIRC sites. n_sites Total number sites. source_params source--parameter mapping. p Total number parameters per site joint model.

what: Leave-one-out cross-validation via Rasmussen & Williams (2006) shortcut — loo_cv
title: Leave-one-out cross-validation via Rasmussen & Williams (2006) shortcut — loo_cv
text: Computes closed-form LOO-CV predictions NOAA sites using block version Rasmussen & Williams (2006) eq. 5.12. site k observation block B_k, LOO predictive mean covariance computed precision matrix without refitting.

title: Leave-one-out cross-validation via Rasmussen & Williams (2006) shortcut — loo_cv
code: loo_cv(model, ...)  # S3 method for class 'evfuse_model' loo_cv(model, loo_source = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'evfuse_naive_model' loo_cv(model, ...)

title: Leave-one-out cross-validation via Rasmussen & Williams (2006) shortcut — loo_cv
text: model fitted model (evfuse_model evfuse_naive_model). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). loo_source data source evaluate LOO-CV (default: first source source_params, typically "NOAA").

title: Leave-one-out cross-validation via Rasmussen & Williams (2006) shortcut — loo_cv
text: evfuse_loo object components: loo_mean Matrix (n_sites x 3) LOO predictive means. loo_cov List 3x3 LOO predictive covariance matrices. observed Matrix (n_sites x 3) observed Stage 1 MLEs. sites Data frame site metadata. loo_lpd Vector per-site log predictive densities.

title: Summarize LOO-CV results — loo_summary
text: Computes RMSE, MAD, return level metrics LOO predictions.

title: Summarize LOO-CV results — loo_summary
Summarize LOO-CV results — loo_summary
text: loo evfuse_loo object. r Return period return level comparison (default 100).

title: Summarize LOO-CV results — loo_summary
text: list parameter-level return-level accuracy metrics.

what: Mann-Kendall trend test with Sen's slope — mann_kendall_test
title: Mann-Kendall trend test with Sen's slope — mann_kendall_test
text: Tests monotonic trend time series using Kendall's tau statistic. Implements test directly rank correlations (external package required). Sen's slope median pairwise slopes.

title: Mann-Kendall trend test with Sen's slope — mann_kendall_test
Mann-Kendall trend test with Sen's slope — mann_kendall_test
text: annual_maxima Numeric vector annual maxima. years Numeric vector corresponding years (length).

title: Mann-Kendall trend test with Sen's slope — mann_kendall_test
text: list components: tau Kendall's tau statistic. S Mann-Kendall S statistic. p_value Two-sided p-value (normal approximation). sens_slope Sen's slope estimate (units per year). sens_intercept Sen's intercept estimate.

what: Pack parameters into a single vector for optim — pack_params

title: Pack parameters into a single vector for optim — pack_params
Pack parameters into a single vector for optim — pack_params

title: Pack parameters into a single vector for optim — pack_params

what: Predict at new locations via universal kriging — predict_krig
title: Predict at new locations via universal kriging — predict_krig
text: Given fitted stage 2 model, predict full parameter vector covariance new spatial locations. extract target components (default: first source's params, e.g. 1-3 NOAA) marginal covariance.

title: Predict at new locations via universal kriging — predict_krig
Predict at new locations via universal kriging — predict_krig
text: model fitted evfuse_model fit_spatial_model. new_sites Data frame lon lat columns prediction locations. predict_params Integer vector parameter indices extract predictions (default: first source's params, .e. 1:3).

title: Predict at new locations via universal kriging — predict_krig
text: list components: pred_mean Matrix (n_new x p) predicted parameter means. pred_cov List n_new pxp predictive covariance matrices. noaa_mean Matrix (n_new x length(predict_params)) predicted target GEV parameters (mu, log_sigma, xi). noaa_cov List n_new marginal covariances target params.

Predict at new locations from a naive model — predict_krig_naive

title: Verify analytic gradient against numerical finite differences — verify_gradient

Verify analytic gradient against numerical finite differences — verify_gradient
text: par Parameter vector check . fn Objective function (scalar-valued). gr Gradient function (returns vector length par). eps Finite difference step size (default 1e-5).

text: List components: analytic Analytic gradient vector. numeric Numerical gradient vector. rel_err Relative error component. max_rel_err Maximum relative error across components.

what: Wendland 2 compactly supported covariance function — wendland2
title: Wendland 2 compactly supported covariance function — wendland2
text: Evaluates Wendland order-2 (C4) function: d (0, 1), C(d) = (1 - d)^6 * (35d^2 + 18d + 3) / 3. Returns 0 d > 1.

title: Wendland 2 compactly supported covariance function — wendland2
Wendland 2 compactly supported covariance function — wendland2
text: d Non-negative distance (scaled range parameter).

title: Wendland 2 compactly supported covariance function — wendland2
text: Covariance value.

title: Wendland 2 taper correlation matrix — wendland_taper_matrix
text: Constructs sparse taper matrix based Wendland C4 function compact support distance lambda.

title: Wendland 2 taper correlation matrix — wendland_taper_matrix
Wendland 2 taper correlation matrix — wendland_taper_matrix
text: D Distance matrix. lambda Taper range (units D). Correlations zero beyond distance.

title: Wendland 2 taper correlation matrix — wendland_taper_matrix
text: Sparse taper matrix (class "dgCMatrix" Matrix package).
